content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DIRECTION; MemoryRouter v3 decision policy separates facts, notes, tasks, and noise; The `MemoryRouter v3` prompt routes transient or low-value content to `noop`, explicit facts/preferences/status/deadlines/commitments to `direct_structured_write`, rationale/trade-offs/design direction/plans/conclusions/lessons/document digests to `note_write`, and combined structured plus contextual material to `mixed`. Its hard rules emphasize preferring `noop` over memory pollution, not treating every question as a task, not forcing rationale into claims, honoring explicit remember requests, and returning valid JSON only.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 1. Memory Router v3`, lines 76-92
- note hot-path:note:<1> DIRECTION; Semantic rules prevent memory pollution in v3 prompt pack; The common semantic rules in `03. Prompt Pack` prioritize memory quality: prefer `noop` over low-value memory, prefer explicit statements over inference, prefer newer explicit statements over older inferred ones, convert relative time to absolute time, keep one reusable unit per memory item, avoid storing hypotheticals as facts, distinguish requests from facts, constrain generalization with `scope_json`, and do not present provisional notes as hard facts.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 0. Общие правила для всех memory prompts`, lines 40-50
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Claim Reconciler v3 claim lifecycle policy; `Claim Reconciler v3` runs after `ClaimExtractor` to compare candidate claims against existing claims and choose update operations: `insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, or `noop`. It uses predicate rules for replace/coexist/range_split, allows coexistence when scope differs by time, project, person, or modality, prefers supersede over silent overwrite, and uses retract only for clear contradiction or disconfirmation.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 7. Claim Reconciler v3`, lines 492-567
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; Claim Extractor v3 extracts atomic semantic claims; `Claim Extractor v3` is invoked when the router chooses `direct_structured_write` or `mixed`, or by a background consolidator, and extracts precise atomic semantic claims from latest turns. Its contract requires one predicate application per claim, `scope_json` to avoid over-generalization, no forced conversion of soft rationale into claims, preferring zero low-quality claims, and valid JSON only.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 6. Claim Extractor v3`, lines 416-491
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; Common rules for v3 memory prompts; The `03. Prompt Pack` section defines common requirements for all memory prompts: pass runtime context such as `current_time_iso`, `timezone`, `namespace_id`, `source_ids`, relevant latest turns or selected sources, only necessary retrieved context, and prompt-specific inputs like `predicate_catalog_excerpt` or `retrieval_budget`. It also requires structured JSON enforcement, low temperature, strict validation, retry on invalid JSON with validator feedback, and versioned prompt names such as `router_v3` and `claim_extract_v3`.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 0. Общие правила для всех memory prompts`, lines 8-38
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> uses_direct_structured_write_for_content; MemoryRouter v3 uses `direct_structured_write` for explicit facts, stable preferences, clear status changes, deadlines, and commitments.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `1. Memory Router v3`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> adds_time_filters_when_turns_mention; Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 adds time filters when turns mention `now`, `before`, `used to`, `from now on`, `no longer`, deadlines, or relative dates.; scope=Within the 03. Prompt Pack document section for Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3.
- note hot-path:note:<5> DOC_DIGEST; Note Reconciler v2 lifecycle reconciliation policy; `Note Reconciler v2` runs after `NoteConstructor` to compare candidate notes with nearby existing notes and decide lifecycle operations: `insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, or `noop`. Its rules use `noop` for near-duplicates, `update` for clearer versions with the same meaning, `supersede` for replaced directions or plans, keep notes when scope differs, and preserve useful rationale via supersede links instead of deletion.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 5. Note Reconciler v2`, lines 331-415
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> allowed_memory_types; MemoryRouter v3 outputs memory types from `claim`, `note`, `task`, and `profile`.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `1. Memory Router v3`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> requires_relative_time_normalization; The v3 prompt pack requires relative time to be converted into absolute time.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `0. Общие правила для всех memory prompts`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> includes_claims_when_content_is; Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 includes claims when preferences, status, or factual updates are likely.; scope=Within the 03. Prompt Pack document section for Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3.
- note hot-path:note:<6> DIRECTION; Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 query and filter policy; `Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3` retrieval plans should include notes for rationale-heavy or plan-heavy material, claims for preferences/status/factual updates, source retrieval when conflicts or grounding checks matter, and time filters for temporal language such as "now", "used to", "from now on", deadlines, or relative dates. Plans should remain precise, preferring fewer better queries.; scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 2. Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3`, prompt rules, lines 156-168
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> allowed_memory_types; Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 output allows memory types `profile`, `claim`, `note`, `task`, and `source`.; scope=Within the 03. Prompt Pack document section for Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3.
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> prefers_noop_over_memory_pollution; The v3 prompt pack prefers `noop` over creating low-quality memory.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `0. Общие правила для всех memory prompts`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> allowed_actions; Claim Reconciler v3 output actions are `insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, and `noop`.; scope=Document `03. Prompt Pack`, section `7. Claim Reconciler v3`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> uses_mixed_for_content; MemoryRouter v3 uses `mixed` when material contains both structured facts or tasks and richer rationale or context.; scope=Rules stated in document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `1. Memory Router v3`.

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=03. Prompt Pack; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=03. Prompt Pack; reason=Document title safely matches existing DOCUMENT entity.
- mention=ProfileUpdater v3; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=AGENT; name=ProfileUpdater v3; reason=Concrete prompt/agent described in the document; no existing matching entity.
- mention=Task Updater v3; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=AGENT; name=Task Updater v3; reason=Concrete prompt/agent section described in the document; no existing matching entity.
- mention=ActionItemUpdater v3; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=AGENT; name=ActionItemUpdater v3; reason=Mention appears as the prompt persona. It may correspond to Task Updater v3, but equivalence is not certain enough for aliasing without an existing entity.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

Prompt Pack Document source: agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md Document section: 03. Prompt Pack / 8. Profile Updater v3 .. 9. Task Updater v3 Document lines: 568-689 ## 8. Profile Updater v3 ### Когда вызывать После claim/task updates, иногда после note consolidation. ### Цель Пересобрать compact profile как projection. ### Выход ```json { "profile_json": {}, "profile_text": "short compact projection", "reason": "short explanation" } ``` ### Prompt ```text You are ProfileUpdater v3. Goal: Build a compact always-in-context profile projection from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context. Current time: {{current_time_iso}} Namespace: {{namespace_id}} Existing profile: {{existing_profile}} Relevant active claims: {{relevant_claims}} Return JSON: { "profile_json": {}, "profile_text": "short compact projection", "reason": "short explanation" } Rules: - Keep the profile short. - Prefer explicit facts over inference. - Do not stuff rationale notes into the profile unless they are clearly stable and operationally useful. - Return valid JSON only. ``` ## 9. Task Updater v3 ### Когда вызывать Когда turns содержат commitments, deadlines или lifecycle updates задач. ### Выход ```json [ { "action": "insert | update | close | cancel | noop", "target_action_item_id": "uuid-or-null", "task": { "title": "string", "description": "string or null", "status": "open | in_progress | blocked | done | cancelled", "priority": "low | normal | high", "due_at": "ISO-8601 or null", "scope_json": {}, "acceptance_criteria": ["..."], "blockers": ["..."], "related_entity_ids": ["uuid"] }, "reason": "short explanation" } ] ``` ### Prompt ```text You are ActionItemUpdater v3. Goal: Create or update operational tasks from explicit commitments, deadlines, or task lifecycle changes. Current time: {{current_time_iso}} Timezone: {{timezone}} Namespace: {{namespace_id}} Latest turns: {{latest_turns}} Existing tasks: {{existing_tasks}} Return JSON array: [ { "action": "insert | update | close | cancel | noop", "target_action_item_id": "uuid-or-null", "task": { "title": "string", "description": "string or null", "status": "open | in_progress | blocked | done | cancelled", "priority": "low | normal | high", "due_at": "ISO-8601 or null", "scope_json": {}, "acceptance_criteria": ["..."], "blockers": ["..."], "related_entity_ids": ["uuid"] }, "reason": "short explanation" } ] Rules: - A plain user request is not automatically a commitment. - Convert relative deadlines to absolute time when possible. - Prefer one clear task over several vague tasks. - Return valid JSON only. ```

Document section: 03. Prompt Pack / 8. Profile Updater v3 .. 9. Task Updater v3
Document lines: 568-689

## 8. Profile Updater v3

### Когда вызывать
После claim/task updates, иногда после note consolidation.

### Цель
Пересобрать compact profile как projection.

### Выход

```json
{
  "profile_json": {},
  "profile_text": "short compact projection",
  "reason": "short explanation"
}
```

### Prompt

```text
You are ProfileUpdater v3.

Goal:
Build a compact always-in-context profile projection from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Existing profile:
{{existing_profile}}

Relevant active claims:
{{relevant_claims}}

Return JSON:
{
  "profile_json": {},
  "profile_text": "short compact projection",
  "reason": "short explanation"
}

Rules:
- Keep the profile short.
- Prefer explicit facts over inference.
- Do not stuff rationale notes into the profile unless they are clearly stable and operationally useful.
- Return valid JSON only.
```

## 9. Task Updater v3

### Когда вызывать
Когда turns содержат commitments, deadlines или lifecycle updates задач.

### Выход

```json
[
  {
    "action": "insert | update | close | cancel | noop",
    "target_action_item_id": "uuid-or-null",
    "task": {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string or null",
      "status": "open | in_progress | blocked | done | cancelled",
      "priority": "low | normal | high",
      "due_at": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "scope_json": {},
      "acceptance_criteria": ["..."],
      "blockers": ["..."],
      "related_entity_ids": ["uuid"]
    },
    "reason": "short explanation"
  }
]
```

### Prompt

```text
You are ActionItemUpdater v3.

Goal:
Create or update operational tasks from explicit commitments, deadlines, or task lifecycle changes.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Timezone: {{timezone}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Latest turns:
{{latest_turns}}

Existing tasks:
{{existing_tasks}}

Return JSON array:
[
  {
    "action": "insert | update | close | cancel | noop",
    "target_action_item_id": "uuid-or-null",
    "task": {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string or null",
      "status": "open | in_progress | blocked | done | cancelled",
      "priority": "low | normal | high",
      "due_at": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "scope_json": {},
      "acceptance_criteria": ["..."],
      "blockers": ["..."],
      "related_entity_ids": ["uuid"]
    },
    "reason": "short explanation"
  }
]

Rules:
- A plain user request is not automatically a commitment.
- Convert relative deadlines to absolute time when possible.
- Prefer one clear task over several vague tasks.
- Return valid JSON only.
```

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

## 8. Profile Updater v3

### Когда вызывать
После claim/task updates, иногда после note consolidation.

### Цель
Пересобрать compact profile как projection.

### Выход

```json
{
  "profile_json": {},
  "profile_text": "short compact projection",
  "reason": "short explanation"
}
```

### Prompt

```text
You are ProfileUpdater v3.

Goal:
Build a compact always-in-context profile projection from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Existing profile:
{{existing_profile}}

Relevant active claims:
{{relevant_claims}}

Return JSON:
{
  "profile_json": {},
  "profile_text": "short compact projection",
  "reason": "short explanation"
}

Rules:
- Keep the profile short.
- Prefer explicit facts over inference.
- Do not stuff rationale notes into the profile unless they are clearly stable and operationally useful.
- Return valid JSON only.
```

## 9. Task Updater v3

### Когда вызывать
Когда turns содержат commitments, deadlines или lifecycle updates задач.

### Выход

```json
[
  {
    "action": "insert | update | close | cancel | noop",
    "target_action_item_id": "uuid-or-null",
    "task": {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string or null",
      "status": "open | in_progress | blocked | done | cancelled",
      "priority": "low | normal | high",
      "due_at": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "scope_json": {},
      "acceptance_criteria": ["..."],
      "blockers": ["..."],
      "related_entity_ids": ["uuid"]
    },
    "reason": "short explanation"
  }
]
```

### Prompt

```text
You are ActionItemUpdater v3.

Goal:
Create or update operational tasks from explicit commitments, deadlines, or task lifecycle changes.

Current time: {{current_time_iso}}
Timezone: {{timezone}}
Namespace: {{namespace_id}}

Latest turns:
{{latest_turns}}

Existing tasks:
{{existing_tasks}}

Return JSON array:
[
  {
    "action": "insert | update | close | cancel | noop",
    "target_action_item_id": "uuid-or-null",
    "task": {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string or null",
      "status": "open | in_progress | blocked | done | cancelled",
      "priority": "low | normal | high",
      "due_at": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "scope_json": {},
      "acceptance_criteria": ["..."],
      "blockers": ["..."],
      "related_entity_ids": ["uuid"]
    },
    "reason": "short explanation"
  }
]

Rules:
- A plain user request is not automatically a commitment.
- Convert relative deadlines to absolute time when possible.
- Prefer one clear task over several vague tasks.
- Return valid JSON only.
```
